name: Greenholtz v. Inmates of Nebraska Penal and Correctional Complex
question:
Does the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment apply to discretionary parole release determinations made by a state’s board of parole?

(1) If so, did the procedures employed by the Nebraska Board of Parole satisfy the requirements of the Due Process Clause?
description: A case in which the Court held that, although there is no constitutional right to an inmate’s release from prison prior to the expiration of a valid sentence, the specific wording of the Nebraska statute created a constitutionally protected expectation of parole.
